ead.archdesc:
======= RMA02209Cornell University Department of Sociology and Anthropology records14-25-2209Compiled by M. WarrenDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Department of Sociology and Anthropology records
Cornell University. Department of Sociology and Anthropology
Leighton, Alexander H. (Alexander Hamilton), 1908-2007.
14-25-2209
3 cubic feet. 3 cubic feet.
1951-1955. Records, reports, notes, interviews, correspondence. Records for a research training seminar and various other reports. Collection material in English 2079298
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M. Warren
Date completed:
April 1985
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, April 2004
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Records of an interdepartmental research training seminar for the social sciences including proposals; reports; faculty and student notes; project data for an Elmira voting study, 1948; and interviews from a St. Joseph's Hospital, Elmira study, 1951. Also correspondence, research reports, and internal memoranda from the Digby, Nova Scotia, Stirling County Study conducted by Alexander Leighton and Garnet E. McCreary, 1952-1955
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
Series 1: Social Science Research Training Seminar
Series 2: Stirling County Study Records
Cite As:
Cornell University Department of Sociology and Anthropology records, #14-25-2209. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Interviewing in sociology. Sociology -- Methodology. Sociology -- Study and teaching. Anthropology -- Study and teaching. Leighton, Alexander H. (Alexander Hamilton), 1908-2007. Cornell University. Department of Sociology and Anthropology
